Internal Compliance Memo — Gyeonwon Dynamics Co., Ltd. (Korea) — PIPA/FSCMA Review of HR Onboarding, IR FAQs, and Preliminary Note Placement
This note consolidates controls for employment documents, investor-relations scripts, and deal communications; all references here mirror public and already-announced facts and do not introduce MNPI under FSCMA.
Under PIPA, personal information of living individuals must be minimized and processed with purpose limitation; HR and IR teams should avoid combining fields in ways that could indirectly identify a specific person.
HR onboarding packet uses generic labels: Applicant Name:, Resident Number:, Department:, and Bank Info:; example placeholders remain invalid by design (Resident Number: 990101-12X456, Bank Info: 081-00123-ABCDE).
Role mailboxes are used for intake (hr@gy-dyn.example, ir@gy-dyn.example); no personal email addresses are collected in IR or hiring workflows.
Product references in IR materials include Blue Crane Platform and HaneulPay Wallet; these are used as metaphors alongside civic sponsorships (East River Tigers, Busan Waves) and place names (Seoul Tech Park) without linking to any individual.
IR FAQs reiterate only public content from the press note dated 2025-03-10 regarding the Aurora Cells roadmap; no unpublished performance metrics, forward-looking revenue targets, or customer identities are discussed.
FSCMA MNPI-screening: the contemplated convertible note placement remains exploratory and unpriced; no definitive term sheets or binding timetable exist, and staff must treat any negotiation detail as non-discussable until a public filing on the Korea Venture Board (KVB).
Onboarding record for Individual Subject A indicates voluntary union membership in Lighthouse Workers Circle; this special-category data requires explicit PIPA consent and is stored separately from employment decisions.
Resident Number field is masked and tokenized (e.g., 000000-0000000X) by Hanbyeol Data Vault Co. under an outsourcing contract; the mapping key is not accessible to HR and is audited quarterly.
IR staff must route market queries to ir@gy-dyn.example and adhere to the “Public-First Disclosure Protocol,” avoiding casual references to draft terms, counterparties, or any non-public operational incidents.
Our Diversity & Belonging Policy (“Harmony Workplace Charter”) encourages civil dialogue across viewpoints on politics and religion, but IR scripts exclude personal topics and avoid collecting any belief-related information.
Wellness messages (mindfulness breaks, hydration tips, and training camp metaphors) are permissible in employee newsletters; they contain no health diagnoses, treatment data, or biometric records.
Call-center scripts may reference public helplines and community sports sponsorships as goodwill examples; these items are non-identifying and do not constitute PII or MNPI under PIPA/FSCMA.
All place and product metaphors are reviewed quarterly to prevent accidental inference about specific employees or investors; stale facts in IR are retained to avoid creating an impression of selective disclosure.
Action items: confirm consent language for special-category data in the HR portal, refresh IR FAQ citations to match the latest public note, and maintain invalid placeholders in generic forms until final go-live.
